pi_items.source: Code verification: setup_task.sh line 10, export_result.sh line 11
Code verification: setup_task.sh line 11, export_result.sh line 12
Code verification: verifier.py line 55 checks 'if tris > 10000'
Code analysis: verifier.py line 61 checks 'if vol > 50000' for minimal valid skull volume, not 100000
Code verification: verifier.py contains no maximum volume check. Comment on line 53 states 'Skull volume usually 300k-500k mm^3'

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real medical imaging data: CT Cranium (InVesalius Sample 0051), a public dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip containing 108 DICOM slices (512×512 pixels, 2mm thickness, 0.4785mm pixel spacing). The dataset is labeled 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample' and dated June 4, 2002. The task output paths (/home/ga/Documents/edentulous_skull.stl and edentulous_view.png) are correctly specified. The verifier enforces a minimum of 10,000 triangles (verified against code). However, there is a discrepancy: the metadata claims min_volume_mm3=100000, but the verifier only checks vol > 50000 mm³. The max_volume_mm3=600000 claim is unverified as the verifier contains no maximum volume constraint.
